feedback.text: This act could really make a difference! But, while we are at it - why not also add in so that customers are protected from video game publishers deleting or making already digital purchases not accessible after we have bought it. I have allot of video games connected to various account, both online purchases and physical. But in todays landscape the video game publishers and digital store-fronts can just remove the product from my account, or just take down the servers hosting the games. Adding in a clause that when you create and sell a digital product you will have to explicitly tell the customers how long you own the product, and what the end-of-life support plans are for said product. Back in the old days you could always go back to play your game, but todays games seems to be designed to be "alive" for a couple of years, and then they take the servers down and you are not able to play them anymore. I can still play the original Half-life from the 90s, even online, this should be the rule not the exception from modern gaming. Thank you!
